items.title: The UK Is Planning a Social Media Curfew for 16- and 17-Year-Olds
items.summary: The restrictions, which can be turned off, will include a crackdown on “addictive” app features and will be in addition to a total ban on children under 16 accessing platforms like TikTok and YouTube.

items.title: YouTube and X Have Become ‘Gateways’ to Nudify Apps
items.summary: A new study found that social media platforms are referring people to sites where they can create nonconsensual, sexually explicit deepfakes for as little as $1 an image.

items.title: New York Governor Signs First Statewide Data Center Moratorium
items.summary: “We have no choice but to address the challenges created by these massive facilities,” New York governor Kathy Hochul said. The executive order will pause construction for one year.

items.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
items.summary: OpenAI's first hardware device is reported to be a screenless, AI-guided smart speaker that can move. Weird enough for you?

items.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
items.summary: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

items.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
items.summary: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

items.title: Trump scraps threat of 20% fee on Hormuz cargo as US resumes blockade of Iran ports
items.summary: Trump drops a 24-hour-old vow to charge cargo ships for using the Strait as the US continues its battle to break Iran's hold on the waterway.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz 'faultline' exposes weakness of the US-Iran deal
items.summary: Control of the waterway has become a key point of contention between the US and Iran, as a ceasefire deal falters.

items.title: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
items.summary: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".

items.title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
items.summary: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan showed that Wall Street is a major beneficiary of the AI boom, with record revenue driven by surging trading and investment banking.

items.title: Watch: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh testifies to House Financial Services committee
items.summary: In remarks prepared for the appearance, Warsh promised a vigilant fight to return inflation to the Fed's 2% target.

items.title: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
items.summary: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Kagan, Barrett push Supreme Court budget with boosted security in rare testimony
items.summary: Two Supreme Court justices testified side-by-side before Congress on Tuesday, telling lawmakers that they need more money to defend against the persistent rise in threats targeting themselves and their families.   It was a rare showing from Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett, two female justices appointed by presidents of different parties who often…

items.title: 5 takeaways as Supreme Court Justices Barrett and Kagan testify on Capitol Hill
items.summary: Capitol Hill saw an unusual moment on Tuesday as Supreme Court Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan testified before lawmakers. The main reason for their appearance was to make the case for the court’s budget request for the coming year, which includes an increase of roughly $14 million focused on security measures. The hearings…

items.title: Key GOP senator concerned over Blanche involvement in Trump IRS deal
items.summary: Sen. John Cornyn (R-Texas), a key member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, says he is “concerned” about acting Attorney General Todd Blanche’s role in crafting a settlement between President Trump, the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) and Justice Department to shield Trump and his family from IRS audits for years to come. Cornyn said he was…

items.title: Target's problems aren't what you think they are
items.summary: Target's problems aren't what you think they are

items.title: Cattle Prices Are Trapped in Downtrends as Energy Prices Spike on U.S.-Iran Volatility
items.summary: Cattle Prices Are Trapped in Downtrends as Energy Prices Spike on U.S.-Iran Volatility

items.title: Here's What to Expect From Extra Space Storage's Next Earnings Report
items.summary: Here's What to Expect From Extra Space Storage's Next Earnings Report

items.title: Eating chili peppers may raise the risk of one deadly cancer
items.summary: A major review found that people who consumed the most chili peppers had a substantially higher risk of esophageal cancer, though the evidence was less clear for stomach and colorectal cancers. Researchers emphasize that the findings show an association, not proof of cause and effect, and that more research is needed to determine whether moderate consumption carries similar risks.

items.title: Popular weight-loss drugs Ozempic and Wegovy may slow biological aging
items.summary: Researchers found that semaglutide, the active ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy, slowed biological aging markers in adults with HIV, marking the first clinical evidence that the drug may influence human aging. Although the findings are encouraging, scientists say larger studies are needed before concluding that the medication can help people age more slowly.

items.title: NASA's Perseverance just completed a marathon on Mars
items.summary: NASA's Perseverance rover has reached an impressive new milestone on Mars, completing the equivalent of a full marathon by driving 26.2 miles (42.195 kilometers) across the Red Planet. It accomplished the feat in just five years and four months, reaching the distance on its 1,890th Martian day, less than half the time it took the previous record holder, NASA's Opportunity rover.

items.title: A new sort of Alzheimer’s drug shows glimmers of promise
items.summary: Clinical trial results show an experimental drug lowered tau levels in the brain and slowed some memory loss, but the data came with a surprise twist.

items.title: A new map traces the sky’s water highways
items.summary: Atmospheric rivers bring heavy rain and floods, but if they don’t come around, it could mean drought. A new global map reveals little-known pathways.

items.title: A quasar breaks the record for most distant supermassive black hole
items.summary: The Euclid space telescope discovery could help researchers understand how black holes grew so massive so quickly in the early universe.
